findings.evidence: `rg -n "addCircleMulEquiv|symm" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/AddCircle.lean` shows only `addCircleMulEquiv_apply` at lines 130-134 and `addCircleMulEquivMultInt_apply` at lines 144-148, with no `symm` lemma.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic lemma for `(addCircleMulEquiv.symm φ).toAdd`, e.g. its coercion to `𝕜` is `φ.1 0`, and the analogous lemma for `addCircleMulEquivMultInt.symm` characterizing the integer by `n • p = φ.1 0`.
findings.issue: `addCircleMulEquiv` has only the forward `[simp]` lemma; the inverse direction, which sends a deck transformation to its translation constant `φ.1 0`, is not part of the public API.

findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ZMultiples.lean:40-43` contains only `intEquivZMultiples_apply`; there is no corresponding `symm_apply` lemma.
findings.fix: Add inverse API, for example a `[simp]` lemma for `(intEquivZMultiples hp).symm ⟨n • p, _⟩ = n` or a theorem saying `((intEquivZMultiples hp).symm a) • p = (a : G)`.
findings.issue: `intEquivZMultiples` exposes an equivalence but only characterizes application `ℤ → zmultiples p`; users cannot rewrite the inverse on a subtype element without unfolding.

summary: The main equivalences are exposed, but their reverse direction is not characterized, so downstream users cannot recover the period element or integer from a deck transformation without unfolding the definitions.
